sections.paragraph: to pine, from hunger and thirst [“Arab. كَفَنَ to roll up;” see Thes.]; once used of a vine wanting water, followed by עַל; Eze 17:7, “behold this vine, כָּֽפְנָה שָֽׁרָשֶׁיהָ עָלָיו thirsting, extended her roots to him” (Syr. to thirst, followed by ܠ to thirst after something, to desire it; Arab. to want, to suffer want.) Hence-
